description: Add tests verifying that tenant containers spawn successfully, become healthy, and have correct auth file ownership

userStories.title: Test: auth files copied with correct ownership during provisioning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a test that verifies provisioning copies auth files into the tenant home dir with correct ownership and non-empty content.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/integration/tc-auth-files.test.ts
Test provisions a tenant via buildApp (control plane) with mock docker
After provisioning, assert auth-profiles.json exists at ${dataDir}/home/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json
Assert auth-profiles.json is non-empty (size > 0)
Assert .credentials.json exists at ${dataDir}/home/.claude/.credentials.json
Assert .credentials.json is non-empty (size > 0)
Assert the files are readable (fs.access with fs.constants.R_OK passes)
Use a real temp dir for dataDir so actual file I/O is tested
Use real auth files from process.env or create minimal fixture files if env not set
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The control plane provisioning code is in apps/control-plane/src/app-factory.ts. It copies auth files via copyFile() at lines ~139-140. The source paths are homedir()/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json and homedir()/.claude/.credentials.json. For CI where these may not exist, create minimal fixture files in beforeAll. Use the vi.stubEnv + vi.resetModules + dynamic import pattern for DATA_DIR isolation. Pattern: see tests/integration/tc-012.test.ts for provisioning via buildApp.

userStories.title: Test: tenant container spawns, becomes healthy, and auth files are accessible
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test that verifies the full container lifecycle: docker run succeeds, container stays up, and the auth files inside the container are accessible by the agent user.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file: tests/integration/tc-container-health.test.ts
Test uses a REAL docker run (not mocked) against the claw-tenant:latest image
Test is skipped if claw-tenant:latest image does not exist (skip with: if (!imageExists) return)
Test provisions a tenant home dir with real auth files (copy from host homedir or create fixtures)
Runs docker run with the same volume mounts as the control plane would use
Asserts the container starts (docker ps shows the container)
Asserts the container health endpoint at the correct port returns {ok: true} within 30 seconds
Asserts docker inspect shows the container is running (not exited)
Asserts that inside the container, /home/agent/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json exists and is non-empty (run docker exec cat to verify)
Asserts that inside the container, /home/agent/.claude/.credentials.json exists and is non-empty
Cleans up: docker stop + docker rm in afterAll
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This test requires Docker to be available and claw-tenant:latest to be built. Check image exists with: execSync('docker image inspect claw-tenant:latest', {stdio:'pipe'}) - if it throws, skip. Use a unique container name to avoid conflicts: claw-tenant-test-<randomId>. The health server inside the container listens on port 3101 by default — map it to a random host port. The message server listens on port 3100. Use execSync('docker run -d ...') and then poll the health endpoint. Cleanup is critical — always remove the container even if test fails (use try/finally in afterAll). Reference the docker run args from buildDockerRunOptions in apps/control-plane/src/docker-run-options.ts for the correct volume/env structure.

userStories.title: Commit and push with PR
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the new tests committed and pushed to a PR.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm test passes (78+ test files)
Changes committed with descriptive message
Branch pushed to origin
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
